mechanism_narrative: Rising real retail sales (RSAFS) reflect stronger consumer demand, which signals economic expansion and pushes the 10-year yield upward over the following month as investors anticipate tighter monetary policy.
failure_condition: Rank IC between monthly changes in RSAFS and 20-day forward yield changes remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or an increase in RSAFS is not followed by a yield rise in >60% of instances.

mechanism_narrative: A drop in the unemployment rate below 6.0% from its current elevated level signals a tightening labor market, leading to higher wage expectations and inflation, which drives the 10-year yield higher over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE < 6.0% after being above 6.5% for at least 3 months
failure_condition: In triggered instances, the 20-day yield change is non-positive in more than half of cases, or the average yield change is not statistically different from zero (t-test p>0.10).

mechanism_narrative: When inflation (CPIAUCSL) is rising while the federal funds rate remains low, real interest rates become increasingly negative, compelling investors to demand higher nominal yields on 10-year Treasuries over the near term.
failure_condition: The cross-sectional interaction term (CPIAUCSL change * FEDFUNDS level) produces a negative or insignificant coefficient in predicting 20-day yield changes, or Rank IC of the interaction signal is below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained recovery in industrial production (INDPRO) from a trough signals stronger economic activity, which lifts the 10-year yield as the market prices in reduced recession risk and tighter future monetary policy over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: In the 60-trading-day window following each trigger, the yield change is negative more than 60% of the time, or the mean yield change is not significantly positive.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) above 1,000,000 units signal a robust housing sector, which boosts economic growth expectations and pushes the 10-year yield up over the subsequent quarter as demand for credit increases.
failure_condition: At least 5 trigger events occur and the average 60-day yield change is negative or not significantly above zero, or the hit rate (positive yield changes) is below 50%.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous payroll growth (PAYEMS rising) and unemployment decline (UNRATE falling) provide a composite signal of a strengthening labor market, which compels the 10-year yield to rise over the next month due to higher income and inflation expectations.
failure_condition: The joint signal (both proxies moving in expansionary direction) does not reliably precede yield increases: the proportion of positive 20-day yield changes following such signals is ≤0.5.

mechanism_narrative: An increase in real GDP (GDPC1) reflects broad economic expansion, which drives up the 10-year yield over a quarterly horizon as investors anticipate future demand for capital and potential Fed tightening.
failure_condition: Quarterly changes in GDPC1 show a negative or insignificant correlation with 60-trading-day yield changes, or the directional accuracy is below 55% over the available sample.

mechanism_narrative: In a post-recession recovery regime, simultaneous increases in industrial production (INDPRO) and nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) confirm the expansion is broad-based, causing the 10-year yield to rise over the next month as growth expectations solidify.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO month-over-month increase > 0 and PAYEMS month-over-month increase > 0
failure_condition: Following at least 10 triggers, the median 20-day yield change is negative or the fraction of positive changes is not significantly above 0.5 (binomial test p>0.05).
